description: Italian SDVECU OWNER login. JWT cap claim is unreliable for arming commands: cap lists ARMNIGHT but the panel rejects ARMNIGHT1 ("Request ARMNIGHT1 is not valid for Central Unit"); cap omits ARMDAY but the panel accepts ARMDAY1 and produces protomResponse 'B' (PARTIAL_DAY_PERI). The Italian web app's 'partial' button issues ARMDAY1, confirming this is the canonical command for the Italian SDVECU.
scenario_notes: Same physical installation as italy_partial_only.json, but logged in as the primary OWNER (phone-number user) rather than a RESTRICTED sub-user.

OWNER cap set adds non-arming features (BILLS, SCH, UNPROTECT, SECONDINSTALLAD, REGDEVICES, CONTMGRFAQ, CONTMGRDOWN, UPDCUENEXTIME) but the arming-relevant subset is identical to RESTRICTED: ARM, DARM, ARMNIGHT, no ARMDAY, no PERI, no annex.
Empirically verified via HAR + HA log: ARMDAY1 → OK + protom 'B'; ARMNIGHT1 → 400 'not valid for Central Unit'.

SCH service is present and exposes attributes DARM/ARMDAY/ARMNIGHT/ARM/PERI as schedule grammar — these reflect what the panel can be put into via schedules, not what the user can issue as a command. Do not rely on SCH attrs as a command-acceptance oracle.

Conclusion: the JWT cap claim is NOT a reliable oracle for which arming commands the panel accepts. The Interior sub-panel must surface all interior modes regardless of cap content; rely on the resolver's mark_unsupported runtime fallback when the panel rejects a command.

panel_truth.armnight1_error: Request ARMNIGHT1 is not valid for Central Unit
